changes_summary: Addressed FLAG-001 (duplicate asset keys) by adding a new Phase 1 step that widens `onDoubleClickAsset` to also carry the originating clip ID, and stores it alongside `lightboxAssetKey`. The navigation hook now resolves context from the specific clip rather than guessing from the asset key. Addressed FLAG-002 (shared lightbox pipeline too narrow for badge metadata) by moving the shot group indicator outside the shared pipeline entirely — it renders as a sibling overlay in VideoEditorProvider rather than threading through lightboxSharedBuilders → LightboxStateContext → NavigationArrows. Also added `shots` to the hook inputs so display names can be derived from shot data (since PinnedShotGroup only stores shotId). Steps renumbered with the new Phase 1 prepended.
questions: Should navigation include audio track clips, or only visual tracks? (Plan assumes visual-only since audio clips have no meaningful lightbox view.)

When the same asset appears in multiple clips across different tracks, should it appear once in the navigation list (deduplicated by asset key) or once per clip? (Plan assumes deduplicated, keeping the first occurrence in priority order.)
For the visual indicator — is a top-center pill with colored dot + shot name the right UX, or did you have something else in mind (e.g., a colored border around the image, a filmstrip preview)?

success_criteria.criterion: Left/right chevron arrows appear when opening lightbox from video editor timeline (when more than one navigable clip exists)
Navigation order follows priority: same-shot-group clips first, then same-track clips, then other-track clips
Keyboard arrow keys (Left/Right) navigate in the same priority order
Double-clicking duplicate clips (same asset on different tracks/groups) starts navigation from the correct clip context
Visual indicator shows current shot group name and color when viewing a clip that belongs to a pinned shot group
Visual indicator disappears or changes when navigating past shot group boundary
No navigation arrows shown when only one navigable clip exists in the timeline
Existing lightbox functionality (close, download, task details, image/video display, variant selector) unaffected
Shot group indicator uses existing SHOT_COLORS palette via getShotColor() for consistency with timeline UI
Position counter shows current item position in the navigation list (e.g. '3 of 12')
Navigation hook recomputes only when timeline data, shots, or lightbox target changes (useMemo dependencies)
No changes to shared lightbox infrastructure (lightboxSharedBuilders.ts, LightboxStateContext.tsx, NavigationArrows.tsx)
Smooth transition/animation when shot group indicator appears/disappears at group boundaries
Mobile swipe navigation also works with the new navigation list
OverlayEditor double-click path also provides correct clip ID for navigation context

assumptions: Only visual track clips are included in navigation (audio clips are excluded since they have no meaningful lightbox representation).
Same asset appearing in multiple clips is deduplicated in the navigation list — each unique asset key appears once, positioned by its first occurrence in priority order.
'Same shot items' means pinned shot groups from the timeline config, not implicit shot associations from generation metadata.

The shot group indicator renders as a sibling overlay to MediaLightbox in VideoEditorProvider, not threaded through the shared lightbox pipeline. This keeps the change contained to the video-editor module.
The navigation hook lives in the video editor domain (not shared) since it depends on video-editor-specific data structures (PinnedShotGroup, ClipMeta, TimelineData).
Effect layers and text clips are excluded from navigation (they have no meaningful lightbox view).
The `lightboxClipId` parameter is optional — if null (e.g., opened from AssetPanel), the hook falls back to finding the first clip matching the asset key.
